items.prompt:
A class collects survey data and needs to organize it.

Raw data
```
pet
dog
cat
dog
fish
cat
```
Show how the learner would record data with stable categories or attributes.
items.expected_evidence: record data with stable categories or attributes
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner demonstrates record data with stable categories or attributes with bounded and technically valid computing reasoning.

items.prompt:
A small dataset is transformed in a spreadsheet.

Table
```
name,score
Ava,7
Luis,4
Mina,9
```
Rule
```
add a new column called passed where score >= 5
```
Trace the change and show how the learner would explain the rule used to sort or classify data.
items.expected_evidence: explain the rule used to sort or classify data
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner demonstrates explain the rule used to sort or classify data with bounded and technically valid computing reasoning.

items.prompt:
A computed output is produced from earlier data-handling steps.

Pipeline brief
```
collect step counts -> remove blanks -> average by day -> graph results
```
Explain the pipeline and show how the learner would match a simple visualization to the organized information it represents.
items.expected_evidence: match a simple visualization to the organized information it represents
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner demonstrates match a simple visualization to the organized information it represents with bounded and technically valid computing reasoning.

items.prompt: Why is a list of facts not yet organized data?
items.expected_evidence: it is not yet organized data until the information is recorded with a stable structure, rule, or category system.
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner explains the structure coherently and ties the explanation back to it is not yet organized data until the information is recorded with a stable structure, rule, or category system.

items.prompt: What should stay the same while you sort data into groups?
items.expected_evidence: the sorting rule should stay stable so each item is classified according to the same basis.
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner explains the structure coherently and ties the explanation back to the sorting rule should stay stable so each item is classified according to the same basis.

items.prompt:
A learner response in data collection, classification, and simple visualization suggests this breakdown: learner records or views data without a stable organizing structure.

Artifact
```
Use the bounded case description above to point to the exact misunderstanding or unstable dependency
```
Diagnose the likely misconception or structural error. Focus on data-pile reasoning.
items.expected_evidence: record data with stable categories or attributes
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner identifies the dominant breakdown, names the structural issue accurately, and redirects the work toward record data with stable categories or attributes.

items.prompt:
A learner response in data collection, classification, and simple visualization suggests this breakdown: learner changes the classification rule during the task or mixes categories.

Artifact
```
Use the bounded case description above to point to the exact misunderstanding or unstable dependency
```
Diagnose the likely misconception or structural error. Focus on category-drift.
items.expected_evidence: explain the rule used to sort or classify data
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner identifies the dominant breakdown, names the structural issue accurately, and redirects the work toward explain the rule used to sort or classify data.

items.prompt:
The same data idea is presented once as a table and once as a chart.

Table view
```
Mon 12
Tue 15
Wed 10
```
Chart view
```
bar chart with three bars labeled Mon Tue Wed
```
Explain how the learner would match a simple visualization to the organized information it represents across the changed representation.
items.expected_evidence: match a simple visualization to the organized information it represents
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner preserves match a simple visualization to the organized information it represents in a changed computing context or representation.

items.prompt:
A familiar classroom dataset is replaced with weather data.

Weather data
```
day,temp
Mon,68
Tue,71
Wed,65
```
Show how the learner would still avoid mixing unrelated categories or changing the sorting rule mid-task.
items.expected_evidence: avoid mixing unrelated categories or changing the sorting rule mid-task
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner preserves avoid mixing unrelated categories or changing the sorting rule mid-task in a changed computing context or representation.

items.prompt:
After a delay, the learner revisits a new dataset and must explain what changed and what can be inferred.

Delayed-check table
```
student,minutes_read
Ava,20
Luis,35
Mina,15
```
Show how they would record data with stable categories or attributes.
items.expected_evidence: record data with stable categories or attributes
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner still demonstrates record data with stable categories or attributes on a delayed recheck without a blocking recurrence.

items.prompt:
Check symbols, encoding, and representation awareness using this bounded data display before closing data collection, classification, and simple visualization.

Probe display
```
favorite fruit counts shown in a two-column table
```
Determine whether the present error is really an upstream representation issue.
items.expected_evidence: shows whether symbols, encoding, and representation awareness is secure enough to support data collection, classification, and simple visualization
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner response makes it possible to distinguish an upstream gap in symbols, encoding, and representation awareness from a current-node issue in data collection, classification, and simple visualization.

items.prompt:
Check file, device, and input-output workflow awareness using this bounded data display before closing data collection, classification, and simple visualization.

Probe display
```
favorite fruit counts shown in a two-column table
```
Determine whether the present error is really an upstream representation issue.
items.expected_evidence: shows whether file, device, and input-output workflow awareness is secure enough to support data collection, classification, and simple visualization
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner response makes it possible to distinguish an upstream gap in file, device, and input-output workflow awareness from a current-node issue in data collection, classification, and simple visualization.
items.suspected_knowledge_gap_if_incorrect: file, device, and input-output workflow awareness
